customTables.name: Table A: Weather Conditions
entries: Overcast - Flat light may reduce details at longer ranges
Fog or mist - Visibility reduced to as little as <= 1/4 mile
Windy - May affect ranged attacks or skills
Light rain/snow - May reduce visibility to as little as <= 1 mile
Heavy rain/snow - Travel speed reduced by half; visibility reduced to <= 1/4 mile
Thunderstorm/blizzard - Survival check required if unsheltered; possible environmental hazards; visibility reduced to 100 ft
Heatwave/cold snap - Inflicts a fatigue penalty (-1 Power or -1 Reflex)
Unnatural weather - Strange clouds, ash, colored rain, whispers on the wind, etc
Weather shift - Often occurs at the worst possible time

customTables.name: Table B: Hex Feature
entries: Water - Spring, creek, lakelet, tidepool, cenote, seasonal pond
Elevation - Ridge, escarpment, sinkhole field, mesa, terraced hills
Vegetation zone - Ancient tree, dead grove, flower plain, reed marsh, thorn scrub
Stone formation - Hoodoos, basalt columns, granite tors, limestone karst, erratic boulders
Trackway - Natural trail, old road, game path, dried riverbed
Natural boundary - River divide, treeline edge, floodplain margin, dune line
Resource - Ore seam, salt lick, resin grove, peat, clay bank
Landform anomaly - Perfectly round hill, split ridge, sunken valley, leaning strata
Ancient imprint - Fossil bed, petrified forest, glacial striations, dried seabed
Climate feature - Wind corridor, frost hollow, heat sink, fog basin, lightning-prone rise

customTables.name: Table C: Signs & Omens
entries: Echoing horns or chanting originating from no clear source
Fresh tracks crossing the party's path
A corpse (animal or person) with a clue
A dropped item (map piece, charm, letter, tool, token, etc.)
A warning sign (runes, skulls, flag, taboo marker, etc.)
A suspicious guide mark (breadcrumbs into trouble)
A clear invitation to investigate (lit fire, fresh camp, open door, etc.)

customTables.name: Table D: Encounter Check
entries: Low Intensity (20%) - Once per hex (or every 4 hours) - 1-in-5
Low Intensity (20%) - Once per hex (or every 4 hours) - 1-in-5
Low Intensity (20%) - Once per hex (or every 4 hours) - 1-in-5
Low Intensity (20%) - Once per hex (or every 4 hours) - 1-in-5
Low Intensity (20%) - Once per hex (or every 4 hours) - 1-in-5
Standard Intensity (30%) - Once per hex (or every 2 hours) - 1-in-3
Standard Intensity (30%) - Once per hex (or every 2 hours) - 1-in-3
Standard Intensity (30%) - Once per hex (or every 2 hours) - 1-in-3
High Intensity (50%) - Twice per hex (or every 1 hour) - 1-in-2
High Intensity (50%) - Twice per hex (or every 1 hour) - 1-in-2

customTables.name: Table E: Encounter Type
entries: Predator or territorial beast - Use Fantasy Monsters, Appendix A
Predator or territorial beast - Use Fantasy Monsters, Appendix A
Prey animal herd or migration - Use Fantasy Monsters, Appendix A
Hazard - Terrain, weather, collapse, fire, thin ice, rip current, etc
Travelers - May want info, rumors, trade, directions, conflict, etc
Desperate folk - Fleeing, hungry, injured, lost, etc
Faction patrol - Questions, demands, tolls, recruiting, conscripting, etc
Monster or unnatural entity - Use Fantasy Monsters, Appendix A
Discovery encounter - Roll on Table F to determine discovery
Roll twice and combine results - E.g. desperate folk fleeing faction patrol's conscription

customTables.name: Table F: Discovery
entries: Useful resource - Food, water, herbs, material, ores
Vantage point - Reveals 1-3 adjacent hex features under normal conditions
Site - Generate according to the procedure found in Settlements & Sites
Dungeon or underground entrance - Use Appendix III to generate a dungeon or underground structure
Lair - Generate according to the procedure found in Settlements & Sites
Settlement - Generate according to the procedure found in Settlements & Sites
Local knowledge - Warning from a local, a scrawled note, whisper on the wind
Weird phenomenon - Rocks roll uphill, sounds are delayed, fata morgana
Treasure (with strings attached) - Cursed, marked, bait, stolen
A solid lead - Roll Table H + Table I

customTables.name: Table G: Settlement Size
entries: Company town, mine, port, or outpost - 100-500

customTables.name: Table H: Conflicts & Hooks
entries: Monster problem (sometimes not as it seems)
Resource crisis (water, food, fuel, medicine, etc.)
Curse, haunting, or strange dreams visit someone
Crime with consequences (theft, murder, sabotage, etc.)
Bad map, false guide, or a lie about the safe route
A bad deal was made (and now payment is due)
Someone is about to do something unwise (and should be stopped)

customTables.name: Table J: Site Condition
entries: Maintained but tense (low resources, limited manpower, political turmoil, etc.)
Ruined and picked over (collapsed, flooded, overgrown, etc.)
Ruined and picked over (collapsed, flooded, overgrown, etc.)
Recently disturbed or uncovered (fresh digging, recent tracks, etc.)
Actively contested (by two or more groups)
Trapped or warded (the signs of which are most likely obvious)

customTables.name: Table K: Occupants & Opposition
entries: No occupants—just hazards (see Appendix IV: Catalogue of Traps)
Powerful monster presence (or a lieutenant with reinforcements)

customTables.name: Table L: Rewards & Treasures
entries: Trade goods (fine fabrics, preserved meats, salt, spices, etc.)
Relic (minor magical or otherwise strange object)
Armor, weapons, or otherwise combat-oriented items
Favor, title, safehouse, or protection from a local faction
Secret knowledge (enemy weakness, good travel route, ritual, etc.)
Key item (importance should be apparent, and it may be needed later)
Treasure that causes trouble (wanted, cursed, marked, stolen, etc.)

customTables.name: Embarking - Region Roll
entries: Random region with major geographical feature (river, mountain, etc.)

customTables.name: Embarking - Hex Roll
entries: Hex near a notable feature (coast, mountain, river, etc.)

customTables.name: Appendix I: Quick Adventure Subroutine
entries: Twist - Use Table J (or Table E: roll twice and combine)
Twist - Use Table J (or Table E: roll twice and combine)
Add a Timer - If not resolved by tomorrow/nightfall/3 days from now...

customTables.name: Appendix II: Plains, Steppe, and Savanna Encounters
entries: Grass fire, slow (1-6) or fast (7-10) moving
Nomad camp; evil (1-2), neutral (3-8), or good (9-10)
Riders approaching at speed (1-4) or mounted scouts watching from afar (5-10)

customTables.name: Appendix II: Forests & Jungles Encounters
entries: Snare line or other primitive trap (see Appendix IV)
Old game path swallowed by roots
Hunting party (1-in-5 chance they are bandits in disguise)
Sudden terrain hazard (falling tree, hidden ravine, mudslide, etc.)

customTables.name: Appendix II: Hills & Mountains Encounters
entries: Mine entrance (1-in-3 chance of fresh prints at the entrance)
Hermit, exile, or oracle (1-in-2 chance they will impart a prophecy)
Something is spotted in the distance

customTables.name: Appendix II: Deserts & Wastelands Encounters
entries: The remains of a caravan, picked over (1-in-2 chance something valuable was left behind)

customTables.name: Appendix II: Swamps & Wetlands Encounters
entries: Predator lurking beneath the water or within the dense trees
Predator lurking beneath the water or within the dense trees
Predator lurking beneath the water or within the dense trees

customTables.name: Appendix II: Encounter Tension or Complication
entries: Terrain slows or complicates movement (mud, loose gravel, crumbling rock, collapsing ground, shifting sands, etc.)
A third party is observing from concealment
The encounter site has a strategic advantage (high ground, choke point, cover, etc.)
A hidden hazard activates (trap, sinkhole, rockfall, swarm, etc.)
A natural resource is nearby, but is actively being contested by two or more groups
The party is in a territorial creature's domain
An omen occurs (potentially forcing a morale check)

customTables.name: Appendix III: Plains Terrain Generator
entries: Same as before, with notable geography

customTables.name: Appendix III: Forest Terrain Generator
entries: Same as before, with notable geography

customTables.name: Appendix III: Hills Terrain Generator
entries: Same as before, with notable geography

customTables.name: Appendix III: Dry Lands Terrain Generator
entries: Same as before, with notable geography

customTables.name: Appendix III: Wetlands Terrain Generator
entries: Same as before, with notable geography

customTables.name: Appendix III: Mountains Terrain Generator
entries: Same as before, with notable geography

customTables.name: Appendix IV: Catalogue of Traps
entries: Deadfall - T3-8; A weighted log, rock slab, or timber frame falls when triggered; inflicts 2-3 damage; target knocked prone
Hidden pit - T4-6; 10-30 ft. deep; may contain spikes; inflicts 1 damage per 10 ft. (or 1d5 + 5 damage if spiked)
Poison dart - T5-6; triggered by tripwire or pressure plate; inflicts 1-2 damage and -1 Power for 1d5 hours unless saved against
Snare - T4-8; target hoisted and restrained
Collapsing floor - T6; triggered by pressure in the middle of the trap; appx. 10 ft. radius; inflicts 1d5 damage
Gas vent - T7; invisible vapor fills a 15 ft. radius; inflicts 1-2 damage per round for 1d3-1d5 rounds
Alarm ward - T6; emits audible, magical, or visible signal; immediately triggers an Encounter, Encounter Tension, or advances the Faction Clock
Rolling boulder - T5; triggered by pressure plate or intrusion threshold in passage; inflicts 1d5 damage; those who fail save are pinned and take 1 damage per round until freed

Execution mechanism - T4-7; designed explicitly to kill (guillotine blade, crushing stone ceiling, impaling spikes, sudden drop into deep chasm, etc.); on failed save, the target is reduced to 0 HP immediately; on a successful save, the target suffers 1d5 + 5 damage
Warding sigil - T4-8; triggered by proximity, tampering, or entry; inflicts 1d5 damage and one of the following effects: 1. Fear; 2. Blindness; 3. Muteness; 4. Paralysis; 5. Marked

customTables.name: Appendix V: Dungeon Micro-engine - Step 1: Dungeon Size
entries: Mega-site fragment - 50+ areas (often only partly accessible)

customTables.name: Appendix V: Step 2A - Doors
entries: Sturdy locked door (T8-10; iron-bound, stone slab, etc.)

customTables.name: Appendix V: Step 2C - Rooms
entries: Ritual chamber, often housing an artifact or strange object

Appendix VII: Quick NPC Generator - Motivation
Appendix VII: Quick NPC Generator - Complication
Appendix VII: Quick Villain Generator - Archetype
Appendix VII: Quick Villain Generator - Ambition
Appendix VII: Quick Villain Generator - Method
Appendix VII: Quick Villain Generator - Flaw
Appendix VII: Time Pressure Generator
entries: Roll again and amplify result (regional consequence implied)

customTables.name: Appendix VII: Solo Play - Fate & Fortune
entries: No, and the answer is amplified
Yes, and the answer is amplified

customTables.name: Appendix VII: Solo Play - Hidden Motives
entries: They need help but are unwilling to admit it
They are lying about something important
